---
title: "Clinical Trial Risk Management Report"
subtitle: "Study STUDY001 — Risk Indicator Summary"
author: "Risk Management Team"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show Code"
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: full
    css: report_styles.css
params:
  snapshot_date: "2026-03-25"
  data_dir: "../data"
  config_dir: "../config"
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import os
import sys
import yaml
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
from IPython.display import display, HTML
from datetime import datetime

# Resolve paths relative to this .qmd file
QMD_DIR = os.path.abspath(".")
DATA_DIR = os.path.join(QMD_DIR, "..", "data")
CONFIG_DIR = os.path.join(QMD_DIR, "..", "config")

# ── Load data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
risk_summary = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "risk_summary.csv"))

with open(os.path.join(CONFIG_DIR, "indicators.yaml"), "r") as f:
    config = yaml.safe_load(f)

# ── Constants ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS_COLORS = {
    "Green": "#2ecc71",
    "Yellow": "#f39c12",
    "Red":    "#e74c3c",
}

SNAPSHOT_DATE = params["snapshot_date"]

qtl_df = risk_summary[risk_summary["indicator_type"] == "QTL"].copy()
kri_df = risk_summary[risk_summary["indicator_type"] == "KRI"].copy()

# ── Indicator metadata ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ind_meta = {}
for group in ("qtl", "kri"):
    for item in config.get(group, []):
        ind_meta[item["id"]] = item

# ── Helper: status badge HTML ──────────────────────────────────────────────
def status_badge(status: str) -> str:
    colors = {"Green": "#2ecc71", "Yellow": "#f39c12", "Red": "#e74c3c"}
    c = colors.get(status, "#aaa")
    return f'<span style="background:{c};color:#fff;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:4px;font-size:0.8rem;font-weight:600;">{status}</span>'

def fmt_table(df: pd.DataFrame) -> str:
    """Render a DataFrame as a styled HTML table."""
    return df.to_html(
        escape=False,
        index=False,
        classes="table table-striped table-hover table-sm",
        border=0,
    )
```

## Executive Summary

```{python}
#| label: exec-summary
#| echo: false

total_sites = risk_summary["SITEID"].nunique()
total_indicators = risk_summary["indicator_id"].nunique()

sites_red    = risk_summary[risk_summary["status"] == "Red"]["SITEID"].nunique()
sites_yellow_only = risk_summary.groupby("SITEID").apply(
    lambda g: (g["status"] == "Yellow").any() and not (g["status"] == "Red").any()
).sum()
sites_green_only = total_sites - sites_red - sites_yellow_only

total_red_flags    = (risk_summary["status"] == "Red").sum()
total_yellow_flags = (risk_summary["status"] == "Yellow").sum()

# Worst sites
worst = (
    risk_summary.groupby("SITEID")
    .apply(lambda g: pd.Series({
        "n_red":    (g["status"] == "Red").sum(),
        "n_yellow": (g["status"] == "Yellow").sum(),
    }))
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values(["n_red", "n_yellow"], ascending=False)
)

worst_site = worst.iloc[0]["SITEID"] if len(worst) > 0 else "N/A"
worst_n_red = int(worst.iloc[0]["n_red"]) if len(worst) > 0 else 0

summary_html = f"""
<div style="background:#f8f9fa;border-left:4px solid #e74c3c;padding:16px 20px;
            border-radius:4px;margin:16px 0;line-height:1.8;">
  <p>As of <strong>{SNAPSHOT_DATE}</strong>, the risk management dashboard covers
  <strong>{total_sites} sites</strong> and <strong>{total_indicators} quality indicators</strong>
  (QTL and KRI) for Study STUDY001.</p>

  <ul>
    <li><strong style="color:#e74c3c;">{sites_red} site(s)</strong> have at least one
    <strong>Red alert</strong> requiring immediate action
    ({total_red_flags} red flag(s) in total).</li>

    <li><strong style="color:#f39c12;">{int(sites_yellow_only)} site(s)</strong> show
    <strong>Yellow alert</strong> status and require close monitoring
    ({total_yellow_flags} yellow flag(s) in total).</li>

    <li><strong style="color:#2ecc71;">{int(sites_green_only)} site(s)</strong> are within
    acceptable limits across all indicators.</li>
  </ul>

  <p>Site <strong>{worst_site}</strong> presents the highest risk profile with
  <strong>{worst_n_red} red alert indicator(s)</strong> and should be prioritised for
  site management intervention.</p>
</div>
"""

display(HTML(summary_html))
```

```{python}
#| label: kpi-cards
#| echo: false

cards_html = f"""
<div style="display:flex;gap:16px;flex-wrap:wrap;margin:16px 0;">

  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:#fff;border-top:4px solid #3498db;
              border-radius:8px;padding:16px;text-align:center;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.08);">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:700;color:#3498db;">{total_sites}</div>
    <div style="color:#7f8c8d;font-size:0.85rem;margin-top:4px;">Total Sites</div>
  </div>

  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:#fff;border-top:4px solid #e74c3c;
              border-radius:8px;padding:16px;text-align:center;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.08);">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:700;color:#e74c3c;">{sites_red}</div>
    <div style="color:#7f8c8d;font-size:0.85rem;margin-top:4px;">Red Alert Sites</div>
  </div>

  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:#fff;border-top:4px solid #f39c12;
              border-radius:8px;padding:16px;text-align:center;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.08);">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:700;color:#f39c12;">{int(sites_yellow_only)}</div>
    <div style="color:#7f8c8d;font-size:0.85rem;margin-top:4px;">Yellow-Only Sites</div>
  </div>

  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:#fff;border-top:4px solid #2ecc71;
              border-radius:8px;padding:16px;text-align:center;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.08);">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:700;color:#2ecc71;">{int(sites_green_only)}</div>
    <div style="color:#7f8c8d;font-size:0.85rem;margin-top:4px;">Green Sites</div>
  </div>

  <div style="flex:1;min-width:140px;background:#fff;border-top:4px solid #9b59b6;
              border-radius:8px;padding:16px;text-align:center;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.08);">
    <div style="font-size:2rem;font-weight:700;color:#9b59b6;">{total_indicators}</div>
    <div style="color:#7f8c8d;font-size:0.85rem;margin-top:4px;">Indicators</div>
  </div>

</div>
"""
display(HTML(cards_html))
```

---

## QTL Overview

Quality Tolerance Limits (QTLs) monitor key quality metrics that, if breached, may
indicate systemic data quality or compliance issues.

### QTL Summary Table

```{python}
#| label: qtl-table

qtl_display = qtl_df[[
    "SITEID", "indicator_id", "indicator_name", "category",
    "value", "threshold_yellow", "threshold_red", "status",
    "n_numerator", "n_denominator",
]].copy()

qtl_display["status_badge"] = qtl_display["status"].apply(status_badge)
qtl_display["value"] = qtl_display["value"].map("{:.2f}%".format)

qtl_display = qtl_display.rename(columns={
    "SITEID": "Site",
    "indicator_id": "ID",
    "indicator_name": "Indicator",
    "category": "Category",
    "value": "Value",
    "threshold_yellow": "Yellow (%)",
    "threshold_red": "Red (%)",
    "status_badge": "Status",
    "n_numerator": "Numerator",
    "n_denominator": "Denominator",
}).drop(columns=["status"])

display(HTML(fmt_table(qtl_display)))
```

### QTL Bar Charts

```{python}
#| label: qtl-bar-charts

for ind_id in sorted(qtl_df["indicator_id"].unique()):
    df_ind = qtl_df[qtl_df["indicator_id"] == ind_id].sort_values("SITEID")
    if df_ind.empty:
        continue

    meta = ind_meta.get(ind_id, {})
    yellow_t = df_ind["threshold_yellow"].iloc[0]
    red_t     = df_ind["threshold_red"].iloc[0]
    bar_colors = [STATUS_COLORS.get(s, "#aaa") for s in df_ind["status"]]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=df_ind["SITEID"],
        y=df_ind["value"],
        marker_color=bar_colors,
        text=[f"{v:.1f}%" for v in df_ind["value"]],
        textposition="outside",
        hovertemplate="<b>%{x}</b><br>Value: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
    ))
    fig.add_hline(y=yellow_t, line_dash="dash", line_color="#f39c12",
                  annotation_text=f"Yellow ({yellow_t}%)",
                  annotation_font_color="#f39c12")
    fig.add_hline(y=red_t, line_dash="dash", line_color="#e74c3c",
                  annotation_text=f"Red ({red_t}%)",
                  annotation_font_color="#e74c3c")
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"{ind_id}: {meta.get('name', '')}",
        xaxis_title="Site",
        yaxis_title="Value (%)",
        height=350,
        margin=dict(l=60, r=80, t=60, b=60),
        showlegend=False,
        template="plotly_white",
    )
    fig.show()
```

---

## KRI Overview

Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) are proactive signals of emerging risks that may affect
study quality, patient safety, or data integrity.

### KRI Summary Table

```{python}
#| label: kri-table

kri_display = kri_df[[
    "SITEID", "indicator_id", "indicator_name", "category",
    "value", "threshold_yellow", "threshold_red", "status",
    "n_numerator", "n_denominator",
]].copy()

kri_display["status_badge"] = kri_display["status"].apply(status_badge)
kri_display["value"] = kri_display["value"].map("{:.2f}%".format)

kri_display = kri_display.rename(columns={
    "SITEID": "Site",
    "indicator_id": "ID",
    "indicator_name": "Indicator",
    "category": "Category",
    "value": "Value",
    "threshold_yellow": "Yellow (%)",
    "threshold_red": "Red (%)",
    "status_badge": "Status",
    "n_numerator": "Numerator",
    "n_denominator": "Denominator",
}).drop(columns=["status"])

display(HTML(fmt_table(kri_display)))
```

### KRI Bar Charts

```{python}
#| label: kri-bar-charts

for ind_id in sorted(kri_df["indicator_id"].unique()):
    df_ind = kri_df[kri_df["indicator_id"] == ind_id].sort_values("SITEID")
    if df_ind.empty:
        continue

    meta = ind_meta.get(ind_id, {})
    yellow_t = df_ind["threshold_yellow"].iloc[0]
    red_t     = df_ind["threshold_red"].iloc[0]
    bar_colors = [STATUS_COLORS.get(s, "#aaa") for s in df_ind["status"]]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=df_ind["SITEID"],
        y=df_ind["value"],
        marker_color=bar_colors,
        text=[f"{v:.1f}%" for v in df_ind["value"]],
        textposition="outside",
        hovertemplate="<b>%{x}</b><br>Value: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
    ))
    fig.add_hline(y=yellow_t, line_dash="dash", line_color="#f39c12",
                  annotation_text=f"Yellow ({yellow_t}%)",
                  annotation_font_color="#f39c12")
    fig.add_hline(y=red_t, line_dash="dash", line_color="#e74c3c",
                  annotation_text=f"Red ({red_t}%)",
                  annotation_font_color="#e74c3c")
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"{ind_id}: {meta.get('name', '')}",
        xaxis_title="Site",
        yaxis_title="Value (%)",
        height=350,
        margin=dict(l=60, r=80, t=60, b=60),
        showlegend=False,
        template="plotly_white",
    )
    fig.show()
```

---

## Site Risk Profile

### Risk Heatmap

The heatmap below summarises the traffic-light status of every indicator for each site.
Red cells indicate a threshold breach requiring immediate action; yellow cells require
enhanced monitoring.

```{python}
#| label: heatmap
#| fig-height: 5

STATUS_NUMERIC = {"Green": 0, "Yellow": 1, "Red": 2}

pivot = risk_summary.pivot_table(
    index="indicator_id", columns="SITEID", values="status", aggfunc="first"
).fillna("Green")

indicators = sorted(pivot.index.tolist())
sites       = sorted(pivot.columns.tolist())

z_num  = pivot.loc[indicators, sites].applymap(lambda s: STATUS_NUMERIC.get(s, 0)).values
z_text = pivot.loc[indicators, sites].values

colorscale = [
    [0.0, "#2ecc71"],
    [0.5, "#f39c12"],
    [1.0, "#e74c3c"],
]

# Build hover text with values
hover = []
for ind in indicators:
    row_hover = []
    for site in sites:
        row_data = risk_summary[(risk_summary["indicator_id"] == ind) & (risk_summary["SITEID"] == site)]
        if not row_data.empty:
            v = row_data["value"].iloc[0]
            s = row_data["status"].iloc[0]
            row_hover.append(f"Site: {site}<br>Indicator: {ind}<br>Value: {v:.1f}%<br>Status: {s}")
        else:
            row_hover.append(f"Site: {site}<br>Indicator: {ind}<br>No data")
    hover.append(row_hover)

fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
    z=z_num,
    x=sites,
    y=indicators,
    text=z_text,
    texttemplate="%{text}",
    colorscale=colorscale,
    zmin=0,
    zmax=2,
    showscale=False,
    hovertext=hover,
    hoverinfo="text",
    xgap=3,
    ygap=3,
))

fig.update_layout(
    title="Site × Indicator Risk Heatmap",
    xaxis=dict(title="Site", side="bottom"),
    yaxis=dict(title="Indicator", autorange="reversed"),
    height=420,
    margin=dict(l=120, r=20, t=60, b=60),
    template="plotly_white",
)

fig.show()
```

### Site Risk Score Table

```{python}
#| label: site-risk-score
#| echo: false

site_risk = risk_summary.groupby("SITEID").agg(
    Red    = ("status", lambda x: (x == "Red").sum()),
    Yellow = ("status", lambda x: (x == "Yellow").sum()),
    Green  = ("status", lambda x: (x == "Green").sum()),
).reset_index()

site_risk["Overall Risk"] = site_risk.apply(
    lambda r: status_badge("Red") if r["Red"] > 0
              else (status_badge("Yellow") if r["Yellow"] > 0
                    else status_badge("Green")),
    axis=1,
)

site_risk = site_risk.rename(columns={
    "SITEID": "Site",
    "Red": "# Red Flags",
    "Yellow": "# Yellow Flags",
    "Green": "# Green",
})

display(HTML(fmt_table(site_risk)))
```

---

## Appendix: Indicator Definitions

```{python}
#| label: appendix
#| echo: false

rows_html = ""
for group_label, group_key in [("QTL — Quality Tolerance Limits", "qtl"),
                                 ("KRI — Key Risk Indicators", "kri")]:
    rows_html += f"""
    <tr>
      <td colspan="7" style="background:#e8f4fd;font-weight:700;color:#2980b9;
                              padding:10px 12px;font-size:0.95rem;">
        {group_label}
      </td>
    </tr>
    """
    for item in config.get(group_key, []):
        direction = item.get("direction", "higher_is_worse")
        dir_label = "Lower is worse" if direction == "lower_is_worse" else "Higher is worse"
        rows_html += f"""
        <tr>
          <td><strong>{item['id']}</strong></td>
          <td>{item['name']}</td>
          <td>{item.get('category', '')}</td>
          <td style="font-size:0.82rem;color:#555;">{item.get('description', '')}</td>
          <td style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:600;">{item.get('threshold_yellow', '')}%</td>
          <td style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:600;">{item.get('threshold_red', '')}%</td>
          <td style="font-size:0.82rem;">{dir_label}</td>
        </tr>
        """

table_html = f"""
<table class="table table-bordered table-sm" style="font-size:0.88rem;">
  <thead style="background:#2c3e50;color:#fff;">
    <tr>
      <th>ID</th>
      <th>Name</th>
      <th>Category</th>
      <th>Description</th>
      <th>Yellow Threshold</th>
      <th>Red Threshold</th>
      <th>Direction</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    {rows_html}
  </tbody>
</table>
"""

display(HTML(table_html))
```

---

*Report generated on `{python} SNAPSHOT_DATE` · Study STUDY001 · Risk Management Team*
